# Cycle 475 Review

## 1. [receipt-integrity] The worklog receipt table mixed a post-cycle fix into a table it claimed was auto-generated through cycle-complete

**File**: docs/worklog/2026-04-11/080114-cycle-475-cycle-474-review-processed-3-findings-1-actioned-via-rollback-2-deferred-chronic-category-rollback-via-3-tool-first-exceptions-pr-2407-merged.md:49-59
**Evidence**: The note says the table was auto-generated by `cycle-receipts --cycle 475 --through 2026-04-11T07:54:10Z`, i.e. through `cycle-complete`. But the table then adds `7ddef72` as `direct-edit (post-cycle-complete, in_flight_sessions counter fix)`. Running `bash tools/cycle-receipts --cycle 475 --repo-root /home/runner/work/schema-org-json-ld/schema-org-json-ld` after fetching full history returns only 6 receipts and stops at `ef94c07`; `git show --no-patch --format='%H %cI %s' 7ddef72` shows that commit landed at `2026-04-11T07:57:56Z`, after the stated cutoff. The docs commit message (`87204dff`) also admits `7ddef72` was added manually. Cycle 474 was already cited for receipt-table drift, so repeating the same category one cycle later is chronic, not incidental.
**Recommendation**: Keep the receipt table strictly machine-scoped to `cycle-receipts` output and move any post-cycle/manual exceptions into narrative prose below the table. If the cycle needs a post-cycle fix called out, add a separate “post-cycle corrections” section rather than mutating the receipt ledger.

## 2. [journal-quality] The journal again marked commitments FOLLOWED after only posting comments, not meeting the promised observables

**File**: docs/journal/2026-04-11.md:72-80
**Evidence**: The carried commitments define concrete observables: PR `#2399` must merge without turning `pipeline-check` red on historical entries, and PR `#2397` must merge and make `bash tools/write-entry worklog --auto-review-summary --dry-run` succeed against real `docs/state.json`. The follow-through then labels both commitments `FOLLOWED` based only on posting revision comments, while explicitly admitting `Copilot had not iterated by close-out so merge/verification moves to cycle 476.` GitHub still shows both PRs (`#2397`, `#2399`) open and draft, so neither observable happened during cycle 475. This is the same observable-vs-status inflation that cycle 474’s review already flagged.
**Recommendation**: Grade commitment follow-through against the observable, not the preparatory step. If the cycle only posted revision requests, mark the commitment partial/deferred and carry forward the unchanged observable text until the merge-time check actually runs.

## 3. [state-integrity] Cycle 475 closed with a `last_cycle` summary that is already contradicted by the live state ledger

**File**: docs/state.json:7249-7255,8068,8339-8345
**Evidence**: `agent_sessions` records cycle-review issue `#2406` as `status: "merged"` with `pr: 2407`, and `dispatch_log_latest` records new cycle-475 review dispatch `#2410`. But `last_cycle.summary` still says `0 dispatches, 0 merges`. Re-running `bash tools/state-invariants` fails invariant 8 on exactly this mismatch: `last_cycle.summary reports 0 dispatches for cycle 475, but dispatch_log_latest also reports cycle 475 activity: #2410`. The stale summary also leaked outward: the generated review issue `#2410` says `PRs merged: None` even though GitHub shows PR `#2407` merged at `2026-04-11T07:39:16Z` and state now tracks that merge.
**Recommendation**: Decide one truth model for post-close-out review activity and enforce it end-to-end. Either (a) treat the review dispatch/merge as cycle-475 activity and update `last_cycle.summary` whenever record-dispatch or reconcile mutates cycle-475 agent sessions after `cycle-complete`, or (b) explicitly exclude that activity from both invariants and generated review summaries. The current half-frozen state leaves the repo red and the generated cycle metadata wrong.

## Complacency score

2/5 — cycle 475 did some visible process work correctly: the issue has step-by-step comments, the worklog is specific about tool gaps, and `metric-snapshot` is still clean. But the cycle repeated two categories the prior review had already called out (receipt integrity and journal truthfulness), and the repository now fails `state-invariants` because the published `last_cycle` summary no longer matches the live ledger. That is not a one-off slip; it is recurring drift in the exact accountability artifacts meant to keep the process honest.
